title: Hub now covers all 5 whitepaper days; M5 composes M4 rather than reimplementing a gate
context: FACT: M1-M4 shipped (loop, tools, memory, quality); M5 is the last day (Prototype->Production). FACT: Day 5's core rule is evaluation-gated deployment — deployment is built ON TOP of evaluation. DECISION D001 set the deep-vertical template; each module is now a known gated motion.
options_considered: M5 reimplements its own pass/fail logic (self-contained, but duplicates M4)
M5 imports M4's evaluator and makes it the deploy gate (composition; demonstrates the Day-5 lesson literally)
decision: M5's deploy_gate.py imports M4's evaluate() (path resolved from __file__) and uses it as the gate: BLOCKED unless every eval case passes, then canary, then human sign-off; evolve() turns prod failures into golden cases. The cross-module import IS the teaching point (deployment composes evaluation) and keeps a single source of truth for 'good'.
consequences: The 5 modules now form a dependency chain (M5->M4) that mirrors the real loop. check.sh O10 runs M5's selftest, which exercises M4 too. Adds a coupling: moving/renaming M4's exercise breaks M5's import — acceptable and intentional (it models real pipeline coupling). Hub now spans learning->production end-to-end.
